question: Should Dennett merge the first durable local project-session schema after reviewing its recovery evidence and bounded limitations?
context: WP-M01-005 introduces the first active-development persistent schema. The embedded Head remains the sole canonical writer; SQLite uses WAL plus synchronous FULL, exact stored-event checksums and explicit payload versions. Automated tests cover terminal and partial recovery, transactional rollback, corruption refusal, schema and migration mismatch, watch resync and child-process restart. Production backup, encryption, physical power-loss and multi-process writer testing remain explicitly outside this package.

options.description: Reject the schema and keep project sessions process-local
benefits: avoids committing the first durable development schema
costs: blocks desktop restart recovery and WP-M01-006 through WP-M01-007
discards the reviewed append-only journal and fault-handling work
risks: later IPC work may invent persistence semantics without the tested Head-owned boundary

options.description: Accept the bounded schema and merge WP-M01-005
benefits: provides deterministic restart recovery for completed and interrupted turns
fails closed on corruption or unknown schema instead of silently losing history
unblocks authenticated desktop IPC and full M01 qualification
costs: future schema revisions must use explicit migrations and preserve recovery compatibility
risks: the bounded development profile does not yet prove production backup, encryption or hardware-fault behavior

reason: The first durable schema is narrow, Head-owned, provider-neutral, independently reviewed and covered by normal, restart, rollback, corruption and stale-stream tests; its remaining production-hardening boundary is explicit.

resolution.note: The owner reviewed the storage behavior, confirmed that the core realistic tests are present, requested deeper cross-project hardening before final release, and explicitly approved WP-M01-005 for merge.
